steps.title: Recognize the y-intercept
steps.content: The point $(0, 9)$ lies on the line. Since $x = 0$ at this point, $y = 9$ is the $y$-intercept.

steps.title: Write the equation
steps.content: With slope $m = 7$ and $y$-intercept $b = 9$, slope-intercept form gives: $$y = 7x + 9$$

steps.title: Match the answer
steps.content: The equation with slope $7$ and $y$-intercept $9$ is $y=7x+9$. That matches Choice A — $y = 7x + 9$.
